analysis.name: Anthony Cirri
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Literally the best professor I've ever had. Would follow him to the ends of the Earth and is the reason I'm studying what I am. Only downside is that his tests are hard asf and you really have to work for your grade even with extra credit and lots of resources and fair grading. Not an easy class at all but 100% worth it

Dr. Cirri is a really good professor and super funny, but his exams are extremely hard. You can do 14+ hours of studying a week and make sure to do the problem sets and still end up with B+. If you enjoy a funny professor during lectures, then he might be for you. If you care about your gpa, don't take his classes.

Dr. Cirri was by far one of the best professors I have had in a STEM course. He was extremely passionate about the subject and gave amazing lectures! The content and tests are hard but he's a very fair grader and prepares you extremely well. If you want to truly understand the material, he is definitely the best professor for you!

Honestly hes a great professor overall i did enjoy his class its just that he gives you so much information in such a short amount of time and your expected to just remember everything,100pgs from lecture a week, 20pg problem sets weekly that are very difficult. Honestly the hardest class ive ever taken. Expect to study 10-12 hours a week minimum

The professor is very good in the sense that you just have to read the ~100 lecture slides every week and do the ~30 pages of problem sets he gives every week. If you can memorize every word on both of these documents, you are guaranteed at least a C+, like me. Median is ~B-, nothing to worry about, especially if pre-med!

review_highlights.comment: Doctor Andrew's MATH1070 is disappointing. Lectures are simplistic and rely on distracting group work, while exams are unreasonably hard. The gap is huge with poor support. Even with AP Calc BC experience, I struggled. This approach fails entry-level students. Do whatever you can to pass the placement exam and skip Penn's intro mandatory math.
Genuinely the worst professor I've ever had. Avoid him at all costs.
Lectures were not great but the textbook explained well and the class was manageable.

Rude, did not help students to succeed, actively worked against the class' wants for more study materials. 
The worst grade me or my peers have received in any course at Penn, and certainly not for a lack of trying.

my experience was very unpleasant. he doesn't explain things clearly and his teaching method did not fit my style of learning. as student's we were forced to teach ourselves through dense and boring text book readings then go to class. he is simply a teacher who does not TEACH. it was impossible for me to pass. he is abstract which doesn't work.

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Amy Stornaiuolo and her TA make the class engaging and fun, with creative and up-to-date topics. Assignments are diverse and allow multiple formats beyond traditional readings. Guest speakers are occasionally invited, which adds a lot. I really enjoyed this class.
Dr. Amy is patient, caring and professional.

Amy is a great teacher. Just that she gives you quite a lot of work, which is very manageable. As long as you do the work and the readings you will get an A. She is also a task master and expects the best from her students. She is also available after class and will help.

analysis.name: Andrew Lamas

review_highlights.comment: Lamas is interested in real critical social work - not anti-intellectualism. Some students became frightened when he threw out the syllabus - if you are at school simply for the "credentials" then save space in the class for those who will actually benefit. I will reference Lamas and the work we did probably forever.
Outside the box thinker who enables you to pursue your intellectual agenda!

Expectations were not clear and constantly changing.  He was insulting and condescending, and though I liked the way he encouraged us to do real-wold policy projects, the fact that he threw out our syllabus left me totally unprepared for future coursework in policy.  By the time he started warming up to us, it was too late.  Worst class I ever had.

This is an awesome class if you are interested in learning, especially free-form learning. Andy has many resources, and is a creativeflexible instructor. He is also demanding: he expects you to bring as much passion to the work as he does. I highly recommend his class if you want to cultivate your intellectual awareness and opinions of the world

This class offers plenty of interesting material, some flexibility. Its often engaging;sometimes a bit overwhelming. Professor Lamas pulls in incredibly interesting sources; at times it seems like too much, at other times it seems like a miracle to encounter such gems. Discussions can be feisty; students are encouraged to take risks.

 Would absolutely take another class of his!

she uses the class to sell her textbook.
